construct: Demonstrates proficient use of listening, speaking, reading and writing skills in real-life situations, adapting to audience and purpose to communicate effectively and support life long learning.

elementsOfConstruct.description: Demonstrates masterly of written language conventions

subSkills.label: Uses words with orthographic accuracy; applies grammar/punctuation; figurative language; writes continuous/non-continuous texts

elementsOfConstruct.description: Interprets, summarises and translates continuous and non-continuous texts

subSkills.label: Reads and understands texts; accurate word use for audience; translates; summarises in writing
Identifies main ideas; analyses purpose/tone; follows instructions; interprets spoken source→target

elementsOfConstruct.description: Expresses ideas, thoughts and opinions through spoken language

subSkills.label: Presents ideas logically; speaks clearly; appropriate vocabulary/tone for purpose and audience

components.structureNotes: Shared scheme across language codes 1211–1217 (Lunda, Luvale, Kiikaonde, Silozi, Chitonga, Icibemba, Cinyanja). SBA 30% / FE 70%. Use subjectCode for the specific language paper when generating.

rules: One assessment scheme covers codes 1211–1217; load by language code when available.

exemplars.scenarioTheme: Reading, summary and translation scenario in a Zambian language community context
